headline: Drug Policy Reform Debate Intensifies After 2,400 Overdose Deaths
context: Malaysia recorded 2,400 drug overdose deaths in 2025 amid ongoing debate over harm reduction versus enforcement approaches.

cards.big: 2,400 drug overdose deaths in 2025 while 40,000 users remain incarcerated
cards.sub: Punitive approach costs RM2.1 billion annually without reducing drug mortality

cards.big: Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 prescribes mandatory death penalty for trafficking offenses
cards.sub: 2023 amendment allowing judicial discretion has been applied in only 18% of eligible cases

cards.big: Only 22 needle exchange points operate nationally versus estimated need of 200
cards.sub: Harm reduction services cover less than 15% of injection drug users

cards.big: Portugal decriminalized personal drug use in 2001 reducing overdose deaths by 80%
cards.sub: Treatment-focused approach saved more lives than decades of criminalization

cards.big: Jailing people for addiction while they die of overdose serves neither justice nor health
Should Malaysia decriminalize personal drug use and redirect funding to treatment programs
